label: 124a Homeland security information sharing

Receipt of Information From United States Secret Service, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–53, title V, § 502(b)121 Stat. 311Aug. 3, 2007“(1).—In generalThe Under Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis shall receive from the United States Secret Service homeland security information, terrorism information, weapons of mass destruction information (as these terms are defined in Section [sic] 1016 of the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 ()), or national intelligence, as defined in Section [sic] 3(5) of the National Security Act of 1947 () [now ], as well as suspect information obtained in criminal investigations. The United States Secret Service shall cooperate with the Under Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis with respect to activities under sections 204 and 205 of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 [, 124b].6 U.S.C. 48550 U.S.C. 401a(5)50 U.S.C. 3003(5)6 U.S.C. 124a“(2).—Savings clauseNothing in this Act [see Tables for classification] shall interfere with the operation of Section [sic] 3056(g) of Title 18, United States Code, or with the authority of the Secretary of Homeland Security or the Director of the United States Secret Service regarding the budget of the United States Secret Service.”

children.heading: Information sharing
children.label: a Information sharing
children.text: Consistent with , the Secretary, acting through the Under Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis, shall integrate the information and standardize the format of the products of the intelligence components of the Department containing homeland security information, terrorism information, weapons of mass destruction information, or national intelligence (as defined in ) except for any internal security protocols or personnel information of such intelligence components, or other administrative processes that are administered by any chief security officer of the Department.section 485 of this titlesection 3003(5) of title 50

children.heading: Information sharing and knowledge management officers
children.label: b Information sharing and knowledge management officers
children.text: For each intelligence component of the Department, the Secretary shall designate an information sharing and knowledge management officer who shall report to the Under Secretary for Intelligence and Analysis regarding coordinating the different systems used in the Department to gather and disseminate homeland security information or national intelligence (as defined in ).section 3003(5) of title 50

children.heading: State, local, and private-sector sources of information
children.label: c State, local, and private-sector sources of information
A
children.text: establish Department-wide procedures for the review and analysis of information provided by State, local, and tribal governments and the private sector;

children.label: B
children.text: as appropriate, integrate such information into the information gathered by the Department and other departments and agencies of the Federal Government; and

children.label: C
children.text: make available such information, as appropriate, within the Department and to other departments and agencies of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Feedback
children.label: 2 Feedback
children.text: The Secretary shall develop mechanisms to provide feedback regarding the analysis and utility of information provided by any entity of State, local, or tribal government or the private sector that provides such information to the Department.

children.heading: Training and evaluation of employees
children.label: d Training and evaluation of employees
A
children.text: the definitions of homeland security information and national intelligence (as defined in ); andsection 3003(5) of title 50

children.label: i
children.text: might qualify as homeland security information or national intelligence; and

children.label: ii
children.text: might be relevant to the Office of Intelligence and Analysis and the intelligence components of the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: on an ongoing basis, evaluate how employees of the Office of Intelligence and Analysis and the intelligence components of the Department are utilizing homeland security information or national intelligence, sharing information within the Department, as described in this subchapter, and participating in the information sharing environment established under ; andsection 485 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: provide to the appropriate component heads regular reports regarding the evaluations under subparagraph (A).
